---
title: "About"
image: sigtuna.jpeg
about:
  template: jolla
  links:
    - icon: instagram
      text: Instagram
      href: https://www.instagram.com/cheekypong
    - icon: twitter
      text: Twitter
      href: https://twitter.com/cheepong89
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/leecheepong/
    - icon: github
      text: Github
      href: https://github.com/cheepong

---

About this blog

I'm a Data Scientist from Singapore who has recently relocated to London. My professional background includes roles at Apple, Singtel, Grab, and the Ministry of Manpower.

Currently, I'm expanding my expertise in machine learning engineering by pursuing an online Master of Science in Computer Science from Georgia Institute of Technology (GATECH).

This blog serves as a chronicle of my journey living in London, while also being a platform for me to share my thoughts and experiences in data science, machine learning, and artificial intelligence.

## Countries Visited

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px
import plotly.colors

# List of countries provided by the user
countries_data = [
    {"Country": "Singapore", "ISO3": "SGP", "Visited": 1},
    {"Country": "United Kingdom", "ISO3": "GBR", "Visited": 1},
    {"Country": "Malaysia", "ISO3": "MYS", "Visited": 1},
    {"Country": "Indonesia", "ISO3": "IDN", "Visited": 1},
    {"Country": "Thailand", "ISO3": "THA", "Visited": 1},
    {"Country": "Cambodia", "ISO3": "KHM", "Visited": 1},
    {"Country": "Vietnam", "ISO3": "VNM", "Visited": 1},
    {"Country": "Brunei", "ISO3": "BRN", "Visited": 1},
    {"Country": "Australia", "ISO3": "AUS", "Visited": 1},
    {"Country": "New Zealand", "ISO3": "NZL", "Visited": 1},
    {"Country": "United States", "ISO3": "USA", "Visited": 1},
    {"Country": "Canada", "ISO3": "CAN", "Visited": 1},
    {"Country": "France", "ISO3": "FRA", "Visited": 1},
    {"Country": "Norway", "ISO3": "NOR", "Visited": 1},
    {"Country": "Germany", "ISO3": "DEU", "Visited": 1},
    {"Country": "China", "ISO3": "CHN", "Visited": 1},
    {"Country": "Japan", "ISO3": "JPN", "Visited": 1},
    {"Country": "Latvia", "ISO3": "LVA", "Visited": 1},
    {"Country": "Lithuania", "ISO3": "LTU", "Visited": 1},
    {"Country": "Estonia", "ISO3": "EST", "Visited": 1},
    {"Country": "Finland", "ISO3": "FIN", "Visited": 1},
    # New countries
    {"Country": "Hong Kong", "ISO3": "HKG", "Visited": 1},
    {"Country": "Taiwan", "ISO3": "TWN", "Visited": 1},
    {"Country": "Sweden", "ISO3": "SWE", "Visited": 1},
    {"Country": "Spain", "ISO3": "ESP", "Visited": 1}
]

# Create a DataFrame
df = pd.DataFrame(countries_data)
df['Visited_Category'] = df['Visited'].astype(str) # Convert to string for discrete coloring

# Calculate statistics
num_visited = len(df)
total_countries_in_world = 195 # Approximation for recognized countries
percentage_visited = (num_visited / total_countries_in_world) * 100

# Print statistics (Quarto will render this as text)
print(f"I have visited **{num_visited}** countries out of approximately **{total_countries_in_world}** in the world ({percentage_visited:.2f}%).")
print("\n") # Add a newline for separation

# Create the choropleth map
fig = px.choropleth(df,
                    locations="ISO3", # Use ISO3 codes
                    locationmode="ISO-3", # Specify ISO-3 location mode
                    color="Visited_Category", # Use the categorical column for color
                    color_discrete_map={'1': plotly.colors.sequential.Blues[5]}, # Map '1' to a specific blue color
                    title="Countries Visited",
                    projection="natural earth",
                    height=500,
                    hover_name="Country", # Display country name on hover
                    hover_data={"ISO3": False, "Visited_Category": False} # Exclude these from hover
                   )
# To remove the color bar for discrete colors, we can set `showlegend=False` in update_layout
fig.update_layout(showlegend=False)

# Update layout for better appearance
fig.update_geos(showocean=True, oceancolor="LightBlue", showland=True, landcolor="LightGrey", showlakes=True, lakecolor="LightBlue")
fig.update_layout(margin={"r":0,"t":50,"l":0,"b":0})

# Display the figure in Quarto
fig.show()


```
